text.title: Subagent prompt file structure and dispatch
text.summary: Every prompt file under `prompts/` follows the same shape so the orchestrator can dispatch subagents uniformly via the `Agent` tool, and the user can audit prompts in version control.

section.title: Required sections in a prompt file

list.item: `Inputs` ŌĆö the parameter block the orchestrator must pass.
`Pre-flight` ŌĆö env vars, state assumptions, reference files to read first.
`Workflow` ŌĆö numbered steps, concrete enough to execute without further clarification.
`Constraints` ŌĆö explicit do-not list (no push, no `--no-verify`, no out-of-scope edits).
`Output` ŌĆö what the subagent returns (report shape, files written, exit codes, last-line marker).
`Done condition` ŌĆö observable check that lets the orchestrator decide success vs failure.

section.reference: Skeleton in the exact required shape.

section.title: Dispatch via the Agent tool
section.p: The orchestrator's spawn message is short: it points the subagent at a prompt file and supplies the per-run parameters. The subagent reads the file with its own Read tool ŌĆö no inline instruction text, no embedded prose copy.

example.code:
Read /home/nero/workspace/projects/faion-net/faion-network/.aidocs/orchestrator/prompts/05-task-executor.md and execute it with these inputs:
feature_folder: /home/nero/workspace/projects/faion-net/faion-network/.aidocs/agent-methodologies/in-progress/F-073-go-error-handling repo_path: /home/nero/workspace/projects/faion-net/faion-network default_branch: main worktree_root: /tmp/wt-F-073-go-error-handling merge_lock: /tmp/faion-network-merge.lock
Return the report shape declared in the prompt file's Output section. Last line of your final message MUST be: done=<feature_id> commit=<short-sha>

list.item: `subagent_type`: `general-purpose` (default), `faion-sdd-executor-agent` (for SDD task execution with built-in lifecycle moves), or `nero-sdd-executor-agent` (NERO-specific).
`model`: `opus` for planning / review / merge resolution; `sonnet` for execution (or recovery retry after a haiku failure); `haiku` for high-volume mechanical edits.
`isolation`: `worktree` for any phase that writes (EXECUTE, FIX, RECAPTURE). Read-only phases (INTAKE, REVIEW, VERIFY) run in the main tree.
`run_in_background`: `true` for parallel waves; the orchestrator uses task notifications to drive the next step.

rule.statement: A prompt file MUST NOT depend on conversation context outside its `Inputs` block. Every fact the subagent needs is either inline, in an explicitly referenced file, or computable from the inputs.
rule.rationale: The subagent has no access to the parent transcript. Any implicit dependency is silent corruption waiting to happen.

section.title: Idempotency
section.p: Read-only subagents (analyzer, reviewer, verifier) MUST be safely re-runnable. Write-side subagents (executor, fix-applier, deployer) are not idempotent and MUST detect partial state ŌĆö an existing worktree at `worktree_root`, a feature already moved past `in-progress/`, an unmerged feature branch ŌĆö and refuse rather than silently double-applying.
